svg.text: Tensor-level Activation Checkpointing · TorchFX 追踪 + 最小重算子图
Conventional (per-module)
• granularity : entire nn.Module
• coarse tradeoff : retain all outputs OR recompute all
• can't preserve cheap-but-used tensors while dropping expensive ones
Manual alternative:
• hand-write fwd + bwd for each layer
• fine-grained but loses autograd ergonomics
• error-prone, hard to maintain under arch iterations
→ V4 has dozens of custom ops (mHC, compressor, indexer, fused ops)
→ neither extreme is practical at the architecture's complexity
✘ 粗：每层要么全存要么全重算
✘ 手写：放弃自动微分便利
V4: tensor-level annotation + TorchFX
developer flow:
1. write forward as usual (PyTorch)
2. annotate target tensors for checkpointing
framework then:
3. TorchFX concrete trace of full graph
4. backward walk → minimal recomputation subgraph
5. insert recomputation just before matching grad calc
runtime:
• free annotated tensor's memory
• reuse original storage pointer (no memcpy)
• track storage pointers → dedup reshape-like aliases
✔ zero runtime overhead vs hand-written · full autograd ergonomics
